{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Human Resources Administrator",
  "description": "Sundry Foods is an integrated food services company founded in 2003 and operating in major cities in Nigeria. We are bringing our now trademark great tasting ready-to-eat food and quality services to thousands of people and institutions daily through its chain and network of restaurants, bakeries and other catering facilities.

We are proud to own and operate one of Africa&rsquo;s best restaurant and bakery chains and we pride ourselves on our ability to consistently deliver original food and service solutions whether in workplaces, schools and colleges, hospitals, joining even remote sites and different cultural environments using our team of young seasoned professionals and an entire workforce with a passion to deliver nothing but the best to every customer.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position:&nbsp;Human Resources Administrator

Job Locations:&nbsp;Port Harcourt - Rivers, Owerri - Imo, and Uyo - Akwa Ibom
Job Type:&nbsp;Full-Time

Description


	The Human Resources Administrators will be responsible for implementing Human Resources management best practices within assigned business divisions and regions. The role holder will:
	Be the first point of contact to receive and resolve all people-related enquiries or complaints within assigned business division or region.
	Manage recruitment, selection, and on-boarding of new hires within the region to ensure appropriate staffing levels.
	Partner with business unit managers to build their leadership capability in the management of their staff and compliance with HR Policies.
	Facilitate regular refresher trainings (and ensure implementation of on-the-job training) to ensure consistency in employees&rsquo; skills and attitude at work.
	Coordinate Staff Engagement programs and Meetings to identify pertinent employee and work-place issues, provide pragmatic solutions and report outcomes.


Requirements


	A Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree with a minimum of second class lower and 4 years&rsquo; experience in Human Resources Management functions in a fast-paced work environment
	Candidate with experience in Recruitment and training preferred.
	Candidate with HR Certification (CIPM, CIPD, etc) will have an added advantage.
	Candidate with process mindset and able to re-engineer processes is desirable.

Human Resources Administrator Job at Sundry Foods




        
    

        
        Sundry Foods is an integrated food services company founded in 2003 and operating in major cities in Nigeria. We are bringing our now trademark great tasting ready-to-eat food and quality services to thousands of people and institutions daily through its chain and network of restaurants, bakeries and other catering facilities.

We are proud to own and operate one of Africa’s best restaurant and bakery chains and we pride ourselves on our ability to consistently deliver original food and service solutions whether in workplaces, schools and colleges, hospitals, joining even remote sites and different cultural environments using our team of young seasoned professionals and an entire workforce with a passion to deliver nothing but the best to every customer.
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Human Resources Administrator

Job Locations: Port Harcourt - Rivers, Owerri - Imo, and Uyo - Akwa Ibom
Job Type: Full-Time

Description


	The Human Resources Administrators will be responsible for implementing Human Resources management best practices within assigned business divisions and regions. The role holder will:
	Be the first point of contact to receive and resolve all people-related enquiries or complaints within assigned business division or region.
	Manage recruitment, selection, and on-boarding of new hires within the region to ensure appropriate staffing levels.
	Partner with business unit managers to build their leadership capability in the management of their staff and compliance with HR Policies.
	Facilitate regular refresher trainings (and ensure implementation of on-the-job training) to ensure consistency in employees’ skills and attitude at work.
	Coordinate Staff Engagement programs and Meetings to identify pertinent employee and work-place issues, provide pragmatic solutions and report outcomes.


Requirements


	A Bachelor’s Degree with a minimum of second class lower and 4 years’ experience in Human Resources Management functions in a fast-paced work environment
	Candidate with experience in Recruitment and training preferred.
	Candidate with HR Certification (CIPM, CIPD, etc) will have an added advantage.
	Candidate with process mindset and able to re-engineer processes is desirable.
    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Logistics Officer",
  "description": "Hoganguards Limited has garnered experience spanning over two decades since its inception in 1981 by dealing with only reputable and well-organized companies and providing security surveillance for major oil companies, diplomatic missions, banks, and various industrial and residential concerns.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Logistics Officer

Job Location: Lagos
Job type: Full-time

Job Description

The Logistics Officer is responsible for coordinating and managing logistics activities to support company operations, including the movement of personnel, equipment, and supplies to ensure efficient and uninterrupted service delivery.

Key Responsibilities


	Coordinate the deployment of staff, equipment, and materials.
	Manage inventory of uniforms, communication devices, and security tools.
	Oversee fleet operations, including vehicle allocation and maintenance.
	Ensure proper documentation and tracking of all logistics activities.
	Liaise with vendors for procurement and timely supply of materials.
	Support emergency operations with prompt logistics response.


Requirements


	HND / Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Logistics, Business Administration, or a related field.
	2 - 5 years of relevant experience (preferably in a security firm).
	Strong organizational, communication, and problem-solving skills.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;recruitment@thehoganorganization.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

Logistics Officer Job at Hoganguards Limited




        
    

        
        Hoganguards Limited has garnered experience spanning over two decades since its inception in 1981 by dealing with only reputable and well-organized companies and providing security surveillance for major oil companies, diplomatic missions, banks, and various industrial and residential concerns.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Logistics Officer

Job Location: Lagos
Job type: Full-time

Job Description

The Logistics Officer is responsible for coordinating and managing logistics activities to support company operations, including the movement of personnel, equipment, and supplies to ensure efficient and uninterrupted service delivery.
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



Key Responsibilities


	Coordinate the deployment of staff, equipment, and materials.
	Manage inventory of uniforms, communication devices, and security tools.
	Oversee fleet operations, including vehicle allocation and maintenance.
	Ensure proper documentation and tracking of all logistics activities.
	Liaise with vendors for procurement and timely supply of materials.
	Support emergency operations with prompt logistics response.


Requirements


	HND / Bachelor’s Degree in Logistics, Business Administration, or a related field.
	2 - 5 years of relevant experience (preferably in a security firm).
	Strong organizational, communication, and problem-solving skills.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply  
        

    
     




        
        

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Call Center Representative",
  "description": "Clinix Healthcare has 40 years of qualitative experience in Medical and Diagnostic services with over 120 centers across Africa. The state-of-the art Diagnostic Centre represents a milestone towards fulfilling Clinix strategic objectives in providing a world class integrated health system that ensures excellence in health and healthcare for all as a globally recognized destination for healthcare. Clinix Healthcare Nigeria is the first investment in West Africa. The Principal investor is Greenlife Pharmaceuticals and Bliss Gvs Pharmaceuticals. Both companies have outstanding repute in the Nigerian Healthcare industry.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Call Center Representative

Job Location:&nbsp;Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description


	We are seeking a professional, energetic, and customer-focused Call Center Representative to join our front-line team.
	The ideal candidate will be responsible for handling inbound and outbound calls, providing excellent patient support, and ensuring a positive experience for all callers.


Responsibilities


	Answer inbound calls from patients, clients, and partners in a professional and courteous manner.
	Schedule appointments, handle enquiries, and provide accurate information about our services, tests, and procedures.
	Make outbound calls for appointment confirmations, follow-ups, and reminders.
	Manage patient complaints and escalate complex issues to supervisors when necessary.
	Update patient records accurately on the CRM/Health Information System.
	Provide product/service information and address billing or insurance-related enquiries.
	Maintain call logs, records, and generate daily reports on call activities.
	Work collaboratively with other departments to ensure seamless patient support.
	Adhere to call center scripts, policies, and healthcare data protection standards.


Qualifications


	Minimum of OND / HND / Bachelor&rsquo;s degree in any relevant field.
	Proven experience as a Call Center Representative (1&ndash;2 years of experience preferred).
	Experience in a healthcare or hospital environment is a strong advantage.
	National Youth Service Corps (NYSC) completed (where applicable).


Requirements &amp; Skills:


	Excellent verbal communication and active listening skills.
	Strong customer service orientation with a friendly and empathetic approach.
	Ability to handle high call volumes and work under pressure.
	Proficient in computer usage and CRM software.
	Good problem-solving skills and attention to detail.
	Ability to speak clearly in English (additional local language is an advantage).
	High level of integrity and confidentiality.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV&nbsp;to:&nbsp;Admin@clinixhealthcare.com.ng&nbsp;and copy:&nbsp;hr@clinixhealthcare.com.ng&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

Call Center Representative Job at Clinix Healthcare




        
    

        
        Clinix Healthcare has 40 years of qualitative experience in Medical and Diagnostic services with over 120 centers across Africa. The state-of-the art Diagnostic Centre represents a milestone towards fulfilling Clinix strategic objectives in providing a world class integrated health system that ensures excellence in health and healthcare for all as a globally recognized destination for healthcare. Clinix Healthcare Nigeria is the first investment in West Africa. The Principal investor is Greenlife Pharmaceuticals and Bliss Gvs Pharmaceuticals. Both companies have outstanding repute in the Nigerian Healthcare industry.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Call Center Representative
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description


	We are seeking a professional, energetic, and customer-focused Call Center Representative to join our front-line team.
	The ideal candidate will be responsible for handling inbound and outbound calls, providing excellent patient support, and ensuring a positive experience for all callers.


Responsibilities


	Answer inbound calls from patients, clients, and partners in a professional and courteous manner.
	Schedule appointments, handle enquiries, and provide accurate information about our services, tests, and procedures.
	Make outbound calls for appointment confirmations, follow-ups, and reminders.
	Manage patient complaints and escalate complex issues to supervisors when necessary.
	Update patient records accurately on the CRM/Health Information System.
	Provide product/service information and address billing or insurance-related enquiries.
	Maintain call logs, records, and generate daily reports on call activities.
	Work collaboratively with other departments to ensure seamless patient support.
	Adhere to call center scripts, policies, and healthcare data protection standards.


Qualifications


	Minimum of OND / HND / Bachelor’s degree in any relevant field.
	Proven experience as a Call Center Representative (1–2 years of experience preferred).
	Experience in a healthcare or hospital environment is a strong advantage.
	National Youth Service Corps (NYSC) completed (where applicable).


Requirements & Skills:


	Excellent verbal communication and active listening skills.
	Strong customer service orientation with a friendly and empathetic approach.
	Ability to handle high call volumes and work under pressure.
	Proficient in computer usage and CRM software.
	Good problem-solving skills and attention to detail.
	Ability to speak clearly in English (additional local language is an advantage).
	High level of integrity and confidentiality.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] and copy: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

  "description": "Tribest is a corporate support solutions provider with a comprehensive range of people outsourcing services. We have a diversified team of management, strategy, banking and capacity development experts working together to provide organizations with the best human resource management solutions. With a team that diverse, we are setting new standards of performance and raising the bar in excellent service delivery.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Social Media Executive

Job Location:&nbsp;Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description


	Develop and implementsocialmedia strategies across LinkedIn, Instagram, Facebook, and X (Twitter).
	Create and manage digital content including blogs, case studies, infographics, and videos.
	Create job vacancy flyer designs for varioussocialmedia platforms to ensure brand consistency.
	Monitor industry trends and competitor activity to optimize campaigns.
	Track and report on performance metrics, providing actionable insights.
	Support lead generation by producing content that highlights outsourcing solutions and success stories.


Requirements


	A Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Marketing, Communications, or related field.
	2-3 years of experience insocialmedia and digital content roles (2B / outsourcing preferred).
	Strong knowledge of LinkedIn marketing and SEO best practices.
	Excellent writing, editing, and storytelling skills.
	Proficiency in tools such as Canva, Adobe Suite, HubSpot, Hootsuite, and Google Analytics.
	Ability to manage multiple projects and meet deadlines.

Social Media Executive Job at Tribest Corporate Support Limited




        
    

        
        Tribest is a corporate support solutions provider with a comprehensive range of people outsourcing services. We have a diversified team of management, strategy, banking and capacity development experts working together to provide organizations with the best human resource management solutions. With a team that diverse, we are setting new standards of performance and raising the bar in excellent service delivery.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Social Media Executive

Job Location: Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Develop and implementsocialmedia strategies across LinkedIn, Instagram, Facebook, and X (Twitter).
	Create and manage digital content including blogs, case studies, infographics, and videos.
	Create job vacancy flyer designs for varioussocialmedia platforms to ensure brand consistency.
	Monitor industry trends and competitor activity to optimize campaigns.
	Track and report on performance metrics, providing actionable insights.
	Support lead generation by producing content that highlights outsourcing solutions and success stories.


Requirements


	A Bachelor’s Degree in Marketing, Communications, or related field.
	2-3 years of experience insocialmedia and digital content roles (2B / outsourcing preferred).
	Strong knowledge of LinkedIn marketing and SEO best practices.
	Excellent writing, editing, and storytelling skills.
	Proficiency in tools such as Canva, Adobe Suite, HubSpot, Hootsuite, and Google Analytics.
	Ability to manage multiple projects and meet deadlines.
    

    

        Click here to Apply

title: Job Openings at Mai Shayi Coffee Roasters Limited
content:
Mai Shayi Coffee Roasters Limited is a premier specialty coffee brand in Nigeria, renowned for its end-to-end involvement in the coffee value chain. From cultivating Arabica coffee in Jos, Plateau state, to meticulous processing and batch-roasting, we promise an unparalleled coffee experience. As a forward-thinking company, our footprint extends from cafes in Lagos and Abuja to our two arabica coffee farms, a cooperative of 121 farmers, and a dedicated roastery supplying premium coffee for both retail and B2B avenues. With our expansion, we seek a skilled and detail-oriented Internal Auditor to ensure the integrity of our financial operations and compliance with legal regulations.

We are recruiting to fill the following positions below:
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



1.) Cleaner (Hospitality & Facility Hygiene)

Job Location: Asokoro, Abuja (FCT)

Employment Type: Full-time

Purpose of the Job


	We are seeking a dedicated and detail-oriented Cleaner to maintain the highest standards of cleanliness and hygiene within our luxury, high-end restaurant and café environments.
	The ideal candidate will ensure that all areas — including dining spaces, restrooms, kitchens, and back-of-house facilities — remain impeccably clean, sanitized, and well organized at all times.
	This role is critical in delivering a safe, elegant, and welcoming atmosphere that supports an exceptional dining and specialty coffee experience for our guests.


The Role


	Custodian of Cleanliness & Brand Standards
	As a Cleaner, you are essential to upholding the premium presentation and hygiene standards that define our brand. Your attention to detail and consistency will directly impact guest comfort, regulatory compliance, and operational excellence.


Key Responsibilities
Cleaning & Hygiene Excellence:


	Ensure all dining areas, kitchen spaces, restrooms, and service areas are cleaned and maintained to the highest hygiene standards.
	Sweep, mop, and polish floors to maintain a pristine appearance.
	Wipe down and sanitize tables, chairs, counters, and furniture regularly.
	Properly clean and disinfect high-touch surfaces such as door handles, railings, and menu holders.


Restroom Maintenance:


	Maintain restrooms in spotless condition by cleaning sinks, mirrors, toilets, urinals, and floors frequently.
	Refill toiletries including soap, hand sanitizers, paper towels, and tissue as needed.
	Ensure restrooms remain fresh, dry, and fully stocked throughout operational hours.


Waste Management & Sanitation:


	Empty trash bins, replace liners, and dispose of waste in designated areas.
	Ensure proper waste segregation in line with hygiene standards.
	Monitor cleaning supply levels and notify management when restocking is required.


Linen & Uniform Care:


	Ensure cleaning cloths, napkins, and towels are washed, dried, and stored properly.
	Handle staff uniforms (where applicable) and assist in maintaining their cleanliness and presentation.


Safety & Maintenance Reporting:


	Address and promptly report maintenance issues such as leaks, clogs, broken fixtures, or safety hazards to management.
	Place caution signs when cleaning wet floors to prevent accidents.
	Store cleaning chemicals and equipment safely after use.


Operational Support:


	Participate in scheduled deep-cleaning exercises.
	Assist with pre-opening and post-closing cleaning routines.
	Perform other related duties assigned by Management.


What we are looking for?


	Previous experience in hospitality, restaurant, or commercial cleaning preferred
	Strong understanding of hygiene, sanitation, and food safety standards
	High attention to detail and commitment to excellence
	Reliable, punctual, and trustworthy
	Physically fit and capable of handling manual tasks
	Professional attitude and respectful communication


Why Join Mai Shayi Coffee?


	Be part of Nigeria’s leading specialty coffee brand
	Work in a structured and professional hospitality environment
	Competitive salary and growth opportunities
	Join a collaborative, quality-driven team


Application Closing Date: 29th April, 2026.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV and cover letter detailing their relevant experience and motivation to: [email protected], [email protected]  and [email protected] using "Cleaner Application" as the subject of the email.

 

 

 2.) Profesional Chef / Sous Chef

Job Location: Nigeria
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description


	Mai Shayi Coffee Roasters Limited is Nigeria’s leading specialty coffee brand, committed to excellence at every stage of the coffee journey—from farm to cup.
	With thriving cafés in Lagos and Abuja, we offer more than just great coffee; we deliver a refined experience where exceptional brews meet warm hospitality and gourmet cuisine.
	Our locations blend the relaxed charm of a coffeehouse with the elegance of fine dining, featuring signature dishes thoughtfully designed to elevate and complement our premium coffee offerings.
	We are currently seeking a professional and highly skilled Chef with proven expertise in fine dining, modern starters, refined plating, and innovative desserts—someone passionate about crafting memorable culinary experiences for our guests.


Key Responsibilities


	Design and execute sophisticated starters and desserts with excellent presentation
	Assist the Executive Head Chef in overseeing kitchen operations and menu development.
	Lead or support kitchen operations with a focus on high-quality, elegant food preparation
	Prepare and execute recipes with precision and creativity.
	Mentor, Supervise and train kitchen staff, ensuring high standards of food quality and safety.
	Collaborate on menu development for seasonal and signature offerings
	Maintain the highest standards of food safety, hygiene, and kitchen organization
	Maintain a clean and organized work environment


What we're looking for:


	Proven experience as a Chef in a high-volume restaurant setting.
	Strong knowledge of culinary techniques and regional cuisines.
	Excellent leadership and communication skills.
	Ability to work under pressure and maintain a positive attitude.
	Passion for food and commitment to excellence.


Requirements


	3 years of experience in a fine dining or upscale restaurant
	Strong skills in plating, pastry, and contemporary culinary techniques
	Creativity, attention to detail, and a passion for excellence
	Ability to work efficiently in a fast-paced, professional kitchen environment
	Culinary certification is an added advantage.
	Nationwide – Must be willing to work across various locations as needed.


What We Offer:


	A vibrant and growing hospitality brand
	Competitive salary and growth opportunities
	A collaborative, quality-driven team environment
	If you’re ready to lead a team and contribute to a vibrant culinary experience, bring your culinary flair to one of the city’s most exciting venues, we’d love to hear from you.


Application Closing Date: 1st May, 2026.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV and cover letter detailing your relevant experience and motivation to: [email protected], [email protected]  and [email protected] using "Professional Chef" as the subject of the email.

 


3.) Barista

Job Location: Nationwide 
Employment Type: Full-time

The Role


	At Mai Shayi Coffee Roaster’s Limited, a Barista is not simply a beverage maker.
	We are seeking a skilled, passionate, and globally minded Barista to join our team and deliver world-class specialty coffee experiences.
	You will be responsible for preparing exceptional coffee and tea beverages with precision and consistency, delivering outstanding hospitality, and maintaining the highest standards of cleanliness, efficiency, and professionalism.
	This role requires technical expertise, attention to detail, speed under pressure, and a genuine passion for specialty coffee.


Key Responsibilities
Specialty Beverage Craftsmanship:


	Expertly prepare and serve espresso-based beverages, pour-overs, manual brews, signature drinks, and specialty teas with precision and consistency.
	Dial in espresso daily, monitor extraction quality, and adjust grind size and yield for optimal flavor.
	Maintain high standards in milk texturing, latte art, and beverage presentation.
	Ensure consistency in taste, temperature, and visual appeal in every cup served.


Equipment Operation & Maintenance:


	Operate and maintain professional espresso machines, grinders, and brewing equipment in line with global best practices.
	Ensure optimal performance of equipment through routine calibration and cleaning.
	Adhere strictly to food safety, hygiene, and workplace safety standards.


Guest Experience & Coffee Education:


	Deliver warm, confident, and professional service at all times.
	Engage guests with knowledge of coffee origins, processing methods, roast profiles, and flavor notes.
	Make informed recommendations tailored to guest preferences.
	Handle guest feedback professionally and contribute to service recovery where necessary.


Café Operations & Team Collaboration:


	Maintain a clean, organized, and well-stocked coffee station at all times.
	Support smooth service flow by working closely with hostesses, servers, kitchen staff, and management.
	Assist with opening and closing procedures, including inventory checks, cleaning schedules, and restocking.
	Contribute to maintaining a premium, luxury café environment.


Continuous Learning & Brand Representation:

	Participate in internal training sessions and certification programs.
	Represent the Mai Shayi Coffee brand with professionalism, confidence, and integrity.

	Prior experience as a Barista is an added advantage.
	Strong understanding of espresso extraction and milk texturing

You are:


	A craft professional
	A guest experience curator

Why Join Mai Shayi Coffee?


	Work with Nigeria’s leading specialty coffee brand
